revision.text:
{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E|region:JP-38|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Imabari, Ehime]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular burial mound in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped burial mounds {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E|region:JP-38|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Imabari, Ehime]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular burial mound in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped burial mounds {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E|region:JP-38|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Imabari, Ehime]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored stone coffins and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[JR Shikoku]] [[Yosan Line]] [[Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48763217]] Wikidata name: Myōkensan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.058719|N|132.930825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376}}; {{ill|Imabari|qid=Q461020}} | region = [[:en:Shikoku region|Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763217]] Wikidata name: Myōkensan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.058719|N|132.930825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376}}; {{ill|Imabari|WD=Q461020}} | region = [[:en:Shikoku region|Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763217]] Wikidata name: Myōkensan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.058719|N|132.930825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376}}; {{ill|Imabari|WD=Q461020}} | region = [[:en:Shikoku region|Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Imabari, Ehime|Imabari, Ehime]] on the island of [[Shikoku]] in [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated [[:en:Granite|Granite]] bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] [[:en:Yosan Line|Yosan Line]] [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)]]

No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|WD=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line}} [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Myōkensan Kofun]][[ja:妙見山古墳 (今治市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q48763217]] Wikidata name: Myōkensan Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.058719|N|132.930825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376}}; {{ill|Imabari|WD=Q461020}} | region = [[:en:Shikoku region|Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Imabari, Ehime|en|Imabari, Ehime|WD=Q461020|lt=Imabari, Ehime}} on the island of [[Shikoku]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|WD=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line}} [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Imabari, Ehime|en|Imabari, Ehime|WD=Q461020|lt=Imabari, Ehime}} on the island of [[Shikoku]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite}} bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|WD=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line}} [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|WD=Q6573259|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/museum/fujiyama/about/ Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.city.imabari.ehime.jp/kanko/spot/?a=168 Imabari city home home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. {{Short description|Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Myōkensan Kofun | native_name =妙見山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Ehime Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Myōkensan Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = 80 | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.058719|N|132.930825|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|WD=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}; {{ill|Imabari|WD=Q461020|1=Imabari, Ehime}} | region = [[:en:Shikoku region|Shikoku region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = 56m | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations =1990-1993 | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|3|31.39|N|132|55|50.97|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|''' Myōkensan Kofun'''|妙見山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Onishi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Imabari, Ehime|en|Imabari, Ehime|WD=Q461020|lt=Imabari, Ehime|1=Imabari, Ehime}} on the island of [[Shikoku]] in {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].
==Overview== The Myōkensan Kofun is located on a hill with an elevation of 80 meters overlooking Itsuki Bay of the [[Seto Inland Sea]]. It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|WD=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|WD=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line|1=Yosan Line}} [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2010.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/210466|title=妙見山古墳 |trans-title= Myōkensan Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2022}}</ref> It is the largest rectangular Kofun in [[Shikoku]].

It is one of a cluster of three keyhole-shaped Kofuns {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which are shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. Preliminary [[archaeological excavation]] of the first tumulus was undertaken from 1990 to 1993. The tumulus's orientated to the east and has a total length 56 meters. It sits of a partially excavated {{ill|Granite|en|Granite|qid=Q41177|lt=Granite|1=Granite}} bedrock and was made with rammed earth. Two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s were found, one in the posterior circular portion (Chamber No.1) and one in the anterior rectangular portion (Chamber No.2). In both cases, the main axis of the burial chambers was set in the east-west direction (west head position) parallel to the main axis of the mound, and {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were found inside. Chamber No.1 measured 10.8 by, 5.6 meters, and 2 meters high and contained a round mark with a diameter of 16 to 18 cm, believed to have been a {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|qid=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}. Chamber No.2 was smaller, but contained an intact Chinese bronze mirror with a diameter of 11.3 cm. Other grave goods included iron swords, iron axes, and an iron harpoon. Thousands of earthenware fragments were unearthed from the foot of the tumulus, most of which are fragments of double-mouthed jars and trapezoidal ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'', which are believed to be from the early Kofun period. No ''[[fukiishi]]'' have been found.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Overall length: 56 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 39 meter diameter x 7 meters high, 2-tier *Constriction width: 25.5 meters ;Anterior rectangular portion: 33 meters wide x 4 meters high, 2-tier
Currently, the tumulus has been covered for preservation, and the ceiling stone has been relocated outside the mound for display. The surrounding area has been developed as the Fujiyama Health and Culture Park, and the {{nihongo|'''Onishi Fujiyama Historical Museum'''|大西藤山歴史資料館||}} displays restored Sarcophaguss and excavated items. The site is located about 25 minutes on foot from [[:en:JR Shikoku|JR Shikoku]] {{ill|Yosan Line|en|Yosan Line|qid=Q575016|lt=Yosan Line|1=Yosan Line}} [[:en:Onishi Station|Onishi Station]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|qid=Q6573259|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ehime)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
